title: Melania Trump Says No to Epstein Rumors and Helps Survivors
summary: Melania Trump, the First Lady, said she has no connection to Jeffrey Epstein, a man who hurt many people. She told reporters at the White House that these rumors should stop. She also asked for Congress to hold hearings for survivors of Epstein's crimes, so they can share their stories. Melania said she only met Epstein briefly in 2000 and did not know about his bad actions. She denied knowing Ghislaine Maxwell, Epstein's friend, and said an old email between them was just polite. Some survivors think her statement is good but wonder what else she will do. Her husband, President Trump, did not know she would talk about this. The article explains that Melania wants to clear her name and support survivors.

keywords.explanation: the wife of the President of the United States
stories that might not be true
people who have been hurt but are still alive
a group of people who make laws in the U.S.
meetings where people talk about important issues
a message sent on a computer

questions.question: Where did Melania Trump make her statement?
Why did Melania Trump ask for hearings?
What did Melania say about an email to Ghislaine Maxwell?
How did some survivors feel about Melania's statement?
options: They liked it but wanted more
questions.correct_answer: They liked it but wanted more

background_read: Jeffrey Epstein was a wealthy man who was accused of hurting many young people. He died in jail. Ghislaine Maxwell is his friend who helped him and is also in jail. The First Lady is the wife of the President and often talks about important issues. Congress is part of the U.S. government that can hold meetings to discuss problems like this.
Article_Structure: WHO: Melania Trump, the First Lady
WHAT: She denied rumors about Jeffrey Epstein and asked for hearings for survivors
WHY: To clear her name and support people hurt by Epstein

perspectives.description: She wants to stop false rumors and help survivors by asking for congressional hearings.
